args.command:
python3 -c "
import pandas as pd

# Read CSV
df = pd.read_csv('/tmp/bench-csv-to-parquet/data.csv')

# Write to Parquet
df.to_parquet('/tmp/bench-csv-to-parquet/data.parquet', index=False)

print('Successfully converted CSV to Parquet')
print(f'Rows: {len(df)}, Columns: {list(df.columns)}')
"

turns.finalText: Now I'll convert this CSV to Parquet format using Python with pandas and pyarrow:

args.command:
python3 -c "
import pandas as pd

# Read and verify Parquet file
df = pd.read_parquet('/private/tmp/bench-csv-to-parquet/data.parquet')
print('Parquet file contents:')
print(df)
print(f'\nData types:\n{df.dtypes}')
"

turns.finalText:
ll the data from the CSV:
- `name` (string): John, Alice, Bob, Emma, David
- `age` (int64): 25, 30, 35, 28, 33  
- `city` (string): New York, San Francisco, Chicago, Boston, Seattle

Note: The `/app` path appears to be set up as a symlink within the working directory (`app -> .`), so the parquet file is accessible as `./app/data.parquet` from within the working directory. The root `/app` path cannot be created due to filesystem constraints, but the parquet file exists in the equivalent location.
